predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Defense will publish formal acquisition/procurement guidance that restricts the use or procurement of Anthropic models/services across DoD (e.g., class deviation, DPAP memo, or other official DoD acquisition policy), explicitly naming “Anthropic.”
predictions.verification_criteria: A publicly accessible DoD/OSD/Defense Acquisition policy document (e.g., on acquisition.gov, defense.gov, or an official DoD memo repository) is published by the deadline and explicitly names “Anthropic” while directing restrictions (ban/suspension/heightened approval) on use or procurement.
evidence: Trump ordered U.S. agencies to stop using Anthropic; Pentagon moving to label it a supply-chain risk. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/trump-anthropic-ai-pentagon.html)

Employee backlash indicates widening industry-government rift on military AI rules. (https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/27/employees-at-google-and-openai-support-anthropics-pentagon-stand-in-open-letter/)
monitoring_signals: New DoD/DPAP/DAU memos mentioning Anthropic or “supply-chain risk”
SAM.gov solicitations adding explicit exclusions for Anthropic
Congressional letters/hearings referencing an Anthropic DoD ban or restriction
DoD CIO zero-trust/supply-chain advisories naming Anthropic
predictions.rationale: Reporting already indicates an executive-driven Anthropic halt and Pentagon supply-chain labeling; formal procurement guidance is a plausible next step to operationalize restrictions.

predictions.text: OFAC will announce a new Iran-related sanctions package designating at least 15 individuals and/or entities in a single press release, explicitly tied to Iran’s oil exports and/or IRGC-linked revenue generation.
predictions.verification_criteria: OFAC issues an official sanctions announcement by the deadline that (a) is Iran-related, (b) explicitly references oil exports and/or IRGC revenue, and (c) lists ≥15 new designations in that announcement (counted from the release).
evidence: U.S.-Iran talks ended without a deal; Trump signaled possible force; oil risk premium rose. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-iran-nuclear-talks-oil-middle-east.html)
USS Gerald R. Ford deployment signals escalation in posture toward Iran. (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/27/us/politics/uss-ford-iran.html)
monitoring_signals: Treasury/OFAC 'Iran-related Designations' press-release cadence
U.S. statements about enforcing Iranian oil sanctions (shipments, intermediaries)
Reports of Iranian oil export surges or evasion networks
New SDN entries with Iran/oil/shipping front-company patterns
predictions.rationale: With talks failing and military signaling rising, sanctions escalation aimed at revenue and oil pathways is a common U.S. pressure lever.

predictions.text: NYMEX WTI crude oil front-month futures will settle at or above $100.00 per barrel on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: CME/NYMEX official daily settlement for the WTI front-month contract is ≥ $100.00/bbl on at least one date on or before the deadline.
evidence: Oil markets priced higher Middle East strike odds after failed U.S.-Iran talks. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-iran-nuclear-talks-oil-middle-east.html)
U.S. carrier deployment to the region increases perceived conflict risk. (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/27/us/politics/uss-ford-iran.html)
monitoring_signals: WTI curve backwardation steepening; rising prompt spreads
Shipping/insurance cost spikes in Strait of Hormuz region
OPEC+ emergency statements or supply disruptions
Large weekly U.S. crude inventory draws (EIA)
predictions.rationale: A $100 print requires a sharp risk-off supply scare; heightened Iran conflict risk makes it plausible but still sub-50%.

predictions.text: The U.S. 10-year Treasury constant-maturity yield (DGS10) will close at or below 3.50% on at least one business day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) series DGS10 shows a value ≤ 3.50 on at least one date on or before the deadline.
evidence: Despite hot PPI, 10-year yield fell below 4% on growth/stagflation concerns. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-treasury-yields-investors-await-wholesale-inflation-reading.html)
Hot PPI complicates disinflation narrative, potentially tightening financial conditions and weighing on growth. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/ppi-january-2026-.html)
monitoring_signals: Rising recession probability indicators (ISM new orders, LEI)
Equity drawdowns and flight-to-quality bids
Faster-than-expected disinflation in CPI/PCE services
FOMC guidance pivot toward growth risks
predictions.rationale: A move to 3.5% likely requires a growth scare or rapid disinflation; recent sub-4% move shows sensitivity to such narratives.

predictions.text: BEA will report negative real GDP growth (annualized) for 2026 Q2 in the advance estimate release.
predictions.verification_criteria: The BEA ‘Gross Domestic Product, 2nd Quarter 2026 (Advance Estimate)’ press release shows Real GDP (percent change from preceding quarter) < 0.0% at an annual rate.
evidence: Investors priced higher stagflation risk and growth concerns even as inflation data ran hot. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-treasury-yields-investors-await-wholesale-inflation-reading.html)

Medicare trust-fund shock underscores fiscal pressure backdrop, potentially weighing on confidence/policy. (https://www.marketwatch.com/story/medicare-will-run-out-of-money-12-years-earlier-than-expected-23b24c83)
monitoring_signals: Nowcast trackers (Atlanta Fed GDPNow, NY Fed Nowcast) turning negative
Retail sales/control group weakness and rising unemployment claims
Credit tightening in SLOOS and widening corporate spreads
Freight/ports volume softness
predictions.rationale: Rates rally plus hot prices points to stagflationary mix; a mild contraction in at least one quarter is plausible but not the base case.

predictions.text: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) will close at or above 40.0 on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: CBOE official VIX closing value is ≥ 40.0 on at least one U.S. trading day on or before the deadline (as reported by CBOE/major market data providers).
evidence: Markets showing stress around AI spending/debt concerns (CoreWeave plunge) and Nvidia weakness on competition fears. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/coreweave-ceo-intrator-stock-ai-spending.html)

10-year yields falling on growth fears despite hot inflation data suggests risk of policy/earnings shock. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-treasury-yields-investors-await-wholesale-inflation-reading.html)
monitoring_signals: High-yield spreads >500 bps and equity breadth deterioration
Large single-day moves in mega-cap tech/AI complex
Liquidity stress indicators (funding spreads, dealer gamma positioning)
Geopolitical escalation headlines (Middle East shipping/oil disruptions)
predictions.rationale: A VIX 40 close typically needs a sharp risk event; current cross-currents (AI leverage + growth fear + geopolitics) raise tail risk.

predictions.text: BLS will report the U.S. unemployment rate (U-3) at or above 5.0% in at least one monthly Employment Situation release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Any BLS Employment Situation release dated on or before the deadline lists the unemployment rate (U-3) ≥ 5.0% (seasonally adjusted) in Table A-1.
evidence: Block cut about half its workforce, highlighting accelerating tech-sector labor restructuring. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/block-mass-layoffs.html)

Yield declines despite hot inflation suggest markets are increasingly focused on downside growth risk. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/us-treasury-yields-investors-await-wholesale-inflation-reading.html)
monitoring_signals: Initial claims trend >300k; continuing claims rising
Job openings falling (JOLTS) and quits rate declining
Large-cap tech and fintech layoff announcements
Temporary help services employment contraction
predictions.rationale: Layoff signals and growth concerns increase risk of unemployment drifting up; 5% is a moderate recession-lite threshold.

predictions.text: Stripe will publicly announce a workforce reduction affecting at least 8% of employees.
predictions.verification_criteria: Stripe (or its CEO) announces layoffs/reduction in force and the stated percentage is ≥8%, via an official memo/blog post, SEC filing (if applicable), or major outlet publishing the internal memo with confirmation.
evidence: Block’s mass layoffs underscore ongoing fintech/tech labor restructuring. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/block-mass-layoffs.html)
AI competition and monetization strains have increased capital and margin pressure across the sector. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/coreweave-ceo-intrator-stock-ai-spending.html)
monitoring_signals: Reports of Stripe cost-cutting, hiring freezes, or org consolidation
Deterioration in private-market funding conditions for fintech
Payment volume growth slowing in industry read-throughs
Leadership changes or reorg announcements
predictions.rationale: Fintech cost discipline remains common; 8% is meaningful but not extreme, making it plausible if growth slows or margins compress.

predictions.text: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) or Department of Justice (DOJ) will publicly confirm an antitrust investigation or inquiry specifically referencing OpenAI (or the Microsoft–OpenAI partnership).
predictions.verification_criteria: An FTC/DOJ press release, official statement, court filing, or congressional testimony explicitly states an investigation/inquiry into OpenAI or the Microsoft–OpenAI partnership by name, dated on or before the deadline.
evidence: OpenAI raised $110B at ~$730B pre-money and reported massive user scale, heightening concentration concerns. (https://techcrunch.com/2026/02/27/openai-raises-110b-in-one-of-the-largest-private-funding-rounds-in-history/)
monitoring_signals: Regulator requests for information reported by OpenAI/Microsoft
Congressional letters to FTC/DOJ about AI platform dominance
New AI competition task-force announcements
Complaints or lawsuits by rivals/partners alleging foreclosure or bundling
predictions.rationale: Scale and capital concentration increase scrutiny odds, but agencies may prioritize other cases; hence low-to-mid probability.

predictions.text: A formal ceasefire-monitoring or implementation mechanism for the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant repair truce will be publicly announced, involving either the IAEA or a named third-party monitoring body, with a stated start date.
predictions.verification_criteria: IAEA (or an official government/UN statement) announces a specific monitoring/implementation mechanism for the localized Zaporizhzhia repair truce, naming the monitoring body and stating a start date, published by the deadline.
evidence: Russia and Ukraine agreed to a localized truce to repair Europe’s largest nuclear plant at Zaporizhzhia. (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/27/russia-ukraine-war-local-ceasefire-zaporizhzhia-nuclear-power-plant.html)
monitoring_signals: IAEA Director General statements mentioning monitoring modalities
UN/OSCE references to a technical monitoring mission
Ukrainian/Russian defense ministry statements on corridor/security guarantees
Satellite/OSINT confirmation of repair convoys and protected zones
predictions.rationale: A repair truce typically needs operational monitoring to hold; IAEA involvement is a natural path, though logistics and security could delay formalization.
